title: A Visit to the Zoo
summary: A mother and her young daughter visit the zoo. They talk about the animals they see and what the child finds most exciting.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Mum, look! Those are the biggest animals I have ever seen!

body.speaker: Mother
body.text: Those are elephants, sweetheart. They are very big, aren't they?

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Can we go closer? I want to see their noses!

body.speaker: Mother
body.text: Those are called trunks, not noses. And yes, we can walk closer.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Oh! The elephant is drinking water with its trunk!

body.speaker: Mother
body.text: That is right. Elephants use their trunks for drinking and picking things up.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: What animal is that over there? It has a very long neck!

body.speaker: Mother
body.text: That is a giraffe. Giraffes have long necks so they can eat leaves from tall trees.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: I love giraffes! Can we see the lions next?

body.speaker: Mother
body.text: Of course. The lion enclosure is on the other side of the path.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Is the lion sleeping? He is not moving.

body.speaker: Mother
body.text: Yes, lions sleep a lot during the day. They are more active at night.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Mum, I am hungry. Can we get some food?

body.speaker: Mother
body.text: Sure. There is a café near the penguin area. We can eat there.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Penguins! Can we see them after lunch?

body.speaker: Mother
body.text: Yes, we can. This has been a wonderful day, hasn't it?

body.speaker: Child
body.text: The best day ever! I want to come back tomorrow!

annotations.phrase: the biggest animals I have ever seen
annotations.meaningVi: những con vật to lớn nhất tôi từng thấy
annotations.pronunciation: /ðə ˈbɪɡɪst ˈænɪməlz aɪ həv ˈɛvə siːn/
annotations.exampleEn: That mountain is the tallest one I have ever seen.
Elephants live in Africa and Asia.
The elephant picked up fruit with its long trunk.
A giraffe can reach the top of a tall tree.
The giraffe's long neck helps it eat from high branches.

annotations.meaningVi: khu vực nhốt thú (trong vườn thú)
annotations.exampleEn: The tiger enclosure has a large outdoor area for the animals.
Owls are active at night and sleep during the day.
Penguins live in cold climates and cannot fly.

annotations.meaningVi: quán cà phê, quán ăn nhẹ
annotations.exampleEn: They stopped at the café for a sandwich and a drink.
The picnic was perfect — what a wonderful day!
I loved the park so much that I want to come back next weekend.

grammarNotes.title: Question tags: aren't they? hasn't it?
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Question tags (đuôi câu hỏi) được dùng để xác nhận thông tin hoặc tìm kiếm sự đồng ý. Nếu câu chính khẳng định, đuôi câu sẽ là phủ định: 'They are very big, aren't they?' / 'This has been a wonderful day, hasn't it?' Nếu câu chính phủ định, đuôi câu sẽ là khẳng định: 'He isn't here, is he?' Giọng lên ở cuối khi thực sự muốn hỏi, giọng xuống khi chỉ muốn xác nhận.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Question tags are short questions added to the end of a statement to ask for agreement or confirmation. If the main clause is positive, the tag is negative: 'They are very big, aren't they?' / 'This has been a wonderful day, hasn't it?' If the main clause is negative, the tag is positive: 'He isn't here, is he?' Rising intonation asks a real question; falling intonation seeks agreement.

grammarNotes.title: So + purpose clause: 'so they can...'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'so' + mệnh đề để giải thích mục đích hoặc lý do: 'Giraffes have long necks so they can eat leaves from tall trees.' Nghĩa là: Hươu cao cổ có cổ dài để chúng có thể ăn lá từ cây cao. 'So' ở đây tương đương với 'in order to' hoặc 'so that'. Đây là cách đơn giản và tự nhiên để giải thích lý do trong tiếng Anh.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'so' + a clause to explain purpose or reason: 'Giraffes have long necks so they can eat leaves from tall trees.' Here, 'so' means 'in order to' or 'so that'. This is a simple and natural way to explain reasons in English at A1–A2 level.

translationVi:
Con: Mẹ ơi, nhìn kìa! Đó là những con vật to lớn nhất con từng thấy!
Mẹ: Đó là những con voi, con yêu. Chúng rất to, phải không?
Con: Mình có thể đến gần hơn không? Con muốn xem cái mũi của chúng!
Mẹ: Cái đó được gọi là vòi, không phải mũi. Và vâng, mình có thể đi gần hơn.
Con: Ồ! Con voi đang uống nước bằng vòi của nó!
Mẹ: Đúng vậy. Voi dùng vòi để uống nước và nhặt đồ vật.
Con: Con vật kia là con gì vậy? Nó có cái cổ rất dài!
Mẹ: Đó là hươu cao cổ. Hươu cao cổ có cổ dài để chúng có thể ăn lá từ những cây cao.
Con: Con thích hươu cao cổ! Tiếp theo mình có thể xem sư tử không?
Mẹ: Tất nhiên rồi. Khu vực sư tử ở phía bên kia của con đường.
Con: Sư tử đang ngủ à? Nó không cử động gì cả.
Mẹ: Đúng vậy, sư tử ngủ nhiều ban ngày. Chúng hoạt động nhiều hơn vào ban đêm.
Con: Mẹ ơi, con đói. Mình có thể đi lấy đồ ăn không?
Mẹ: Được chứ. Có một quán cà phê gần khu vực chim cánh cụt. Mình có thể ăn ở đó.
Con: Chim cánh cụt! Mình có thể xem chúng sau bữa trưa không?
Mẹ: Được, mình có thể. Hôm nay là một ngày tuyệt vời, phải không?
Con: Ngày đẹp nhất từ trước đến nay! Con muốn quay lại vào ngày mai!

questions.prompt: What are the first animals the child sees?
questions.explanation: The child says 'Those are the biggest animals I have ever seen!' and the mother confirms they are elephants.
questions.hint: What animals does the child call the biggest?

questions.prompt: What does the child call the elephant's trunk?
questions.explanation: The child says 'I want to see their noses!' — confusing the trunk for a nose.
questions.hint: What body part does the child think it is?

questions.prompt: What does an elephant use its trunk for?
questions.explanation: The mother says 'Elephants use their trunks for drinking and picking things up.'
questions.hint: The mother explains two uses for the trunk.

questions.prompt: What does the child notice about the giraffe?
questions.explanation: The child says 'It has a very long neck!'
questions.hint: What unusual physical feature does the child comment on?

questions.prompt: Why do giraffes have long necks?
options: To eat leaves from tall trees
questions.explanation: The mother says 'Giraffes have long necks so they can eat leaves from tall trees.'
questions.hint: What reason does the mother give?

options: On the other side of the path
questions.explanation: The mother says 'The lion enclosure is on the other side of the path.'
questions.hint: What does the mother say about the location of the lions?

questions.prompt: Why is the lion not moving?
questions.explanation: The mother says 'Yes, lions sleep a lot during the day.'
questions.hint: What does the mother say lions do during the day?

questions.explanation: The mother says 'They are more active at night.'
questions.hint: What time of day does the mother mention?

questions.prompt: Why does the child want to stop?
options: She wants to see the penguins
questions.explanation: The child says 'Mum, I am hungry. Can we get some food?'
questions.hint: What does the child say she needs?

questions.explanation: The mother says 'There is a café near the penguin area.'
questions.hint: Which animal area is near the café?

questions.prompt: What does the child want to do after the zoo visit?
questions.explanation: The child says 'I want to come back tomorrow!'
questions.hint: What does the child say at the very end of the dialogue?

cloze.template: Child: Mum, look! Those are the biggest animals I have ever seen! Mother: Those are {{b1}}, sweetheart. They are very big, aren't they? Child: Can we go closer? I want to see their noses! Mother: Those are called {{b2}}, not noses. And yes, we can walk closer. Child: Oh! The elephant is drinking water with its trunk! Mother: That is right. Elephants use their trunks for drinking and picking things up. Child: What animal is that over there? It has a very long {{b3}}! Mother: That is a {{b4}}. Giraffes have long necks so they can eat leaves from tall trees. Child: I love giraffes! Can we see the lions next? Mother: Of course. The lion {{b5}} is on the other side of the path. Child: Is the lion sleeping? He is not moving. Mother: Yes, lions sleep a lot during the day. They are more {{b6}} at night. Child: Mum, I am {{b7}}. Can we get some food? Mother: Sure. There is a {{b8}} near the penguin area. We can eat there. Child: Penguins! Can we see them after {{b9}}? Mother: Yes, we can. This has been a wonderful day, hasn't it? Child: The {{b10}} day ever! I want to come back tomorrow!

blanks.explanation: 'Those are elephants' — the first animals they see at the zoo.
'Called trunks' — the correct word for the long nose of an elephant.
'Very long neck' — the distinctive feature of a giraffe.
'A giraffe' — the animal with the long neck.
'Lion enclosure' — the fenced area where the lions live in the zoo.
'More active at night' — lions are nocturnal hunters.
'I am hungry' — the child wants to stop and eat.
'A café' — a small place to buy food and drinks.
'After lunch' — the meal they will have at the café.
'The best day ever' — the child's enthusiastic description of her day at the zoo.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The child wants to come back to the zoo the very next day. Do you think zoos are a good place for animals? What are the benefits and the problems of keeping animals in a zoo?
